content.text: There are two issues with the new `jira.module.ts` — both `@nestjs/bullmq` imports are split across two lines (causing `import/no-duplicates`). I need to merge them into a single import. The other errors are pre-existing in other files. Let me fix the module.

content.content: The file /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/modules/jira/jira.module.ts has been updated successfully.

input.description: Lint only the two changed files

content.text: No errors in either file. Now confirm the full code-quality-check exits cleanly for our two files, and check whether the broader failures are pre-existing.

input.description: Run type-check to confirm no new type errors

input.command:
git add src/modules/jira/jira.module.ts src/modules/app/app.module.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add JiraModule and register in AppModule with 12h poll cron

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
[main e6bfbc9] feat: add JiraModule and register in AppModule with 12h poll cron
 2 files changed, 60 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 backend/src/modules/jira/jira.module.ts

content.text:
Done. Summary of what was done:

**Created** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/modules/jira/jira.module.ts`
- Merged the two separate `@nestjs/bullmq` imports (`BullModule`, `InjectQueue`) into a single import line to satisfy the `import/no-duplicates` ESLint rule (the spec as given had them split)
- Registers all 6 Jira DB models, 3 services, processor, and controller
- Implements `OnModuleInit` to schedule the `poll_installations` repeating BullMQ job every 12 hours on startup

**Modified** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/modules/app/app.module.ts`
- Added `import { JiraModule } from '@modules/jira/jira.module'` in alphabetical order (between `InvitationModule` and `SessionModule`)
- Added `JiraModule` to the imports array after `GithubModule`

Both files lint and type-check cleanly. The other lint errors visible in `code-quality-check` are pre-existing in unrelated Jira service files (prettier formatting, nullish coalescing, array type) and were not introduced by this task.

STATUS: DONE
